description: UE5 save system: USaveGame, SaveGameToSlot, LoadGameFromSlot, async saving, and serialization

entries.title: USaveGame — Basic Save System
entries.description: USaveGame is UE5's built-in serialization framework for saving and loading game data. Create a subclass, define UPROPERTY fields for your data, and use UGameplayStatics to save/load.
entries.whatItIs: USaveGame is UE5's base class for save data containers. You subclass it, add UPROPERTY fields for the data you want to persist, then use UGameplayStatics helper functions to serialize it to disk and deserialize it back.
whenToUse: Persisting player progress, inventory, settings, or world state between play sessions
Simple to medium complexity save needs where a flat data snapshot is sufficient
Prototyping save/load functionality quickly without external libraries
Single-player or per-player save data that maps to named save slots
whenNotToUse: When you need to save/restore full actor state with references between actors — USaveGame does not handle UObject pointers automatically
MMO or server-authoritative games where saves must live in a database rather than on local disk
When your save data is extremely large (hundreds of MB) — consider chunked or streaming approaches instead
When you need versioning, migration, or complex schema evolution across game updates — USaveGame has limited built-in support for that
commonMistakes: Storing raw UObject* or AActor* pointers in the save game — these cannot be serialized and will be null on load. Use soft references or IDs instead.
Forgetting to check DoesSaveGameExist() before loading, leading to null casts
Not using UPROPERTY() on fields — non-UPROPERTY fields are not serialized by USaveGame
Saving every frame or tick instead of at logical checkpoints, causing disk I/O stutters
Hardcoding slot names instead of using a slot management system, making multiple save slots difficult
keyPoints: Subclass USaveGame and add UPROPERTY() fields for everything you want to save
SaveGameToSlot(SaveObject, SlotName, UserIndex): serialize to disk
LoadGameFromSlot(SlotName, UserIndex): deserialize from disk, returns USaveGame*
DoesSaveGameExist(SlotName, UserIndex): check if a save file exists before loading
CreateSaveGameObject(SaveGameClass): create a new instance to populate before saving
Slot name: a string identifier (e.g., "SaveSlot1", "AutoSave"). Maps to a file on disk.
Supports: basic types, arrays, maps, structs, soft references. Does NOT auto-save actor references.

entries.title: Async Saving & Loading
entries.description: For large save files or avoiding hitches, UE5 supports asynchronous save/load operations that don't block the game thread.
entries.whatItIs: Async save/load functions are non-blocking alternatives to SaveGameToSlot/LoadGameFromSlot that perform disk I/O on a background thread and notify you via a delegate when complete.
whenToUse: Autosaving during gameplay where any frame hitch would be noticeable
Loading save data in menus or loading screens without freezing the UI
When save files are large (many MB) and synchronous I/O causes visible stutters
Cloud save implementations where you serialize to memory first, then upload asynchronously
whenNotToUse: Small save files (a few KB) where synchronous save/load completes in under a millisecond
When you need the save data immediately on the next line of code — async requires a callback
During level transitions where the world is being torn down — the callback may fire on a destroyed object
If your game is already paused or in a loading screen and blocking is acceptable
commonMistakes: Not checking bSuccess in the save completion callback — the save can silently fail (disk full, permissions)
Modifying the save object between calling AsyncSave and the callback firing — the data is being read on another thread
Not guarding against the owning object being destroyed before the async callback fires (use weak pointers or check IsValid)
Calling multiple async saves to the same slot simultaneously, causing file corruption
Assuming async load is instant — showing stale UI data instead of waiting for the callback
keyPoints: AsyncSaveGameToSlot(SaveObject, SlotName, UserIndex, Delegate): non-blocking save
Delegates fire on completion — check success/failure in the callback
Use for: autosave during gameplay, loading save files in menus without freezing UI
SaveGameToMemory() / LoadGameFromMemory(): serialize to/from byte arrays for custom storage (cloud saves)
For very complex saves: consider splitting data across multiple save objects/slots
